-- Hoogle documentation, generated by Haddock
-- See Hoogle, http://www.haskell.org/hoogle/


-- | A package for simple, fast radiocarbon calibration
--   
--   Radiocarbon calibration with the intercept method optimised for fast
--   calibration of many dates.
@package currycarbon
@version 0.1.2.0

module Currycarbon.Types

-- | Different calibration algorithms implemented in currycarbon. Currently
--   two distinct implementations are available, although both of them are
--   similar <a>Intercept calibration</a> algorithms. Maybe more algorithms
--   will be added in the future
data CalibrationMethod

-- | A matrix multiplication method generally following <a>this blog post
--   by Martin Hinz</a>. This method is slower and the underlying code more
--   verbose than <a>Bchron</a>, but it has some advantages regarding
--   didactics and the inspection of intermediate data products for
--   debugging. Using this method is thus generally not advisable, except
--   for specific applications, where a more technical insight into C14
--   calibration is needed
MatrixMultiplication :: CalibrationMethod

-- | A fast and reliable calibration algorithm very similar to the
--   implementation in the <a>R package Bchron by Andrew Parnell</a>. This
--   algorithm can be run with a simple normal distribution
--   (<a>NormalDist</a>) or Student's t-distribution (<a>StudentTDist</a>),
--   which is recommended
Bchron :: CalibrationDistribution -> CalibrationMethod
[distribution] :: CalibrationMethod -> CalibrationDistribution

-- | Statistical distributions to be used with the <a>CalibrationMethod</a>
--   <a>Bchron</a>
data CalibrationDistribution

-- | Normal distribution
NormalDist :: CalibrationDistribution

-- | Student's t-distribution.
StudentTDist :: Double -> CalibrationDistribution

-- | number of degrees of freedom
[ndf] :: CalibrationDistribution -> Double

-- | A type to represent years BP. All numbers are positive and describe
--   the distance in years to 1950AD: 3000 = 3000BP = 1050BC
type YearBP = Word

-- | A type to represent years BC or AD. Negative values describe years BC,
--   positive values years AD: -5000 = 5000BC and 1300 = 1300AD
type YearBCAD = Int

-- | A type to represent a range of years
type YearRange = Word

-- | A data type to represent an uncalibrated radiocarbon date
data UncalC14
UncalC14 :: String -> YearBP -> YearRange -> UncalC14

-- | Sample identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_uncalC14Id] :: UncalC14 -> String

-- | C14 age in years BP
[_uncalC14UnCal] :: UncalC14 -> YearBP

-- | C14 standard deviation (one sigma in years)
[_uncalC14Sigma] :: UncalC14 -> YearRange

-- | A data type to represent a year-wise probability density for
--   uncalibrated dates Although technically not correct, we still call
--   this a probability density function (PDF)
data UncalPDF
UncalPDF :: String -> Vector YearBP -> Vector Float -> UncalPDF

-- | Sample identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_uncalPDFid] :: UncalPDF -> String

-- | Years BP
[_uncalPDFUnCals] :: UncalPDF -> Vector YearBP

-- | Probability densities
[_uncalPDFDens] :: UncalPDF -> Vector Float

-- | A data type to represent a calibration curve with <a>YearBP</a>
data CalCurveBP
CalCurveBP :: Vector YearBP -> Vector YearBP -> Vector YearRange -> CalCurveBP

-- | Years calBP
[_calCurveBPCals] :: CalCurveBP -> Vector YearBP

-- | Years BP
[_calCurveBPUnCals] :: CalCurveBP -> Vector YearBP

-- | Standard deviation (one sigma in years)
[_calCurveBPSigmas] :: CalCurveBP -> Vector YearRange

-- | A second data type to represent a calibration curve, here now with
--   <a>YearBCAD</a>
data CalCurveBCAD
CalCurveBCAD :: Vector YearBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD -> Vector YearRange -> CalCurveBCAD

-- | Years calBCAD
[_calCurveBCADCals] :: CalCurveBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Years BCAD
[_calCurveBCADUnCals] :: CalCurveBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Standard deviation (one sigma in years)
[_calCurveBCADSigmas] :: CalCurveBCAD -> Vector YearRange

-- | A data type to represent a calibration curve in a <i>wide</i> matrix
--   form
data CalCurveMatrix
CalCurveMatrix :: Vector YearBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD -> Vector (Vector Float) -> CalCurveMatrix

-- | Row names of the calibration curve matrix: Years BCAD
[_calCurveMatrixUnCals] :: CalCurveMatrix -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Column names of the calibration curve matrix: Years calBCAD
[_calCurveMatrixCals] :: CalCurveMatrix -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Matrix (as a list of columns) with the probability densities
[_calCurveMatrixDens] :: CalCurveMatrix -> Vector (Vector Float)

-- | A data type to represent a year-wise probability density for
--   calibrated dates. Although technically not correct, we still call this
--   a probability density function (PDF)
data CalPDF
CalPDF :: String -> Vector YearBCAD -> Vector Float -> CalPDF

-- | Sample identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_calPDFid] :: CalPDF -> String

-- | Years calBCAD
[_calPDFCals] :: CalPDF -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Probability densities for each year in <a>_calPDFCals</a>
[_calPDFDens] :: CalPDF -> Vector Float

-- | A data type to represent a human readable summary of a calibrated
--   radiocarbon date
data CalC14
CalC14 :: String -> CalRangeSummary -> [HDR] -> [HDR] -> CalC14

-- | Identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_calC14id] :: CalC14 -> String

-- | Summary of the range of the calibrated date
[_calC14RangeSummary] :: CalC14 -> CalRangeSummary

-- | One-sigma high density regions
[_calC14HDROneSigma] :: CalC14 -> [HDR]

-- | Two-sigma high density regions
[_calC14HDRTwoSigma] :: CalC14 -> [HDR]

-- | A data type to store a simple range summary of the calibrated date,
--   including the median age
data CalRangeSummary
CalRangeSummary :: YearBCAD -> YearBCAD -> YearBCAD -> YearBCAD -> YearBCAD -> CalRangeSummary

-- | Start of the two-sigma age range
[_calRangeStartTwoSigma] :: CalRangeSummary -> YearBCAD

-- | Start of the one-sigma age range
[_calRangeStartOneSigma] :: CalRangeSummary -> YearBCAD

-- | Median age
[_calRangeMedian] :: CalRangeSummary -> YearBCAD

-- | End of the one-sigma age range
[_calRangeStopOneSigma] :: CalRangeSummary -> YearBCAD

-- | End of the two-sigma age range
[_calRangeStopTwoSigma] :: CalRangeSummary -> YearBCAD

-- | A data type to represent a high density region of a probability
--   distribution. A high density region is here defined as an age range,
--   within which the respective cummulative probability (e.g. of an
--   calibrated radiocarbon date density curve) is above a certain
--   threshold
data HDR
HDR :: YearBCAD -> YearBCAD -> HDR

-- | Start of the high density region in years calBCAD
[_hdrstart] :: HDR -> YearBCAD

-- | End of the high density region in years calBCAD
[_hdrstop] :: HDR -> YearBCAD
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Currycarbon.Types.CalibrationDistribution
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalibrationDistribution
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Currycarbon.Types.CalibrationMethod
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalibrationMethod
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Currycarbon.Types.UncalC14
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.UncalC14
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.UncalPDF
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalCurveBP
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalCurveBCAD
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalCurveMatrix
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalPDF
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalRangeSummary
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Currycarbon.Types.HDR
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.HDR
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Types.CalC14

module Currycarbon.Calibration.Utils

-- | get the density of a normal distribution at a point x
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; dnorm 1.0 1.0 1.0
--   0.3989423
--   </pre>
dnorm :: Float -> Float -> Float -> Float

-- | get the density of student's-t distribution at a point x
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; dt 1.0 1.0
--   0.15915494
--   </pre>
dt :: Double -> Float -> Float
isOutsideRangeOfCalCurve :: CalCurveBP -> UncalC14 -> Bool

-- | Take an uncalibrated date and a raw calibration curve and return the
--   relevant segment of the calibration curve
getRelevantCalCurveSegment :: UncalC14 -> CalCurveBP -> CalCurveBP

-- | Modify a calibration curve (segment) with multiple optional steps,
--   including interpolation and transforming dates to BC/AD format
prepareCalCurveSegment :: Bool -> CalCurveBP -> CalCurveBCAD
makeBCADCalCurve :: CalCurveBP -> CalCurveBCAD
vectorBPToBCAD :: Vector YearBP -> Vector YearBCAD
interpolateCalCurve :: CalCurveBP -> CalCurveBP
normalizeCalPDF :: CalPDF -> CalPDF
trimLowDensityEdgesCalPDF :: CalPDF -> CalPDF

module Currycarbon.Utils

-- | Different exceptions for currycarbon
data CurrycarbonException

-- | An exception to describe an issue in the currycarbon CLI input parsing
CurrycarbonCLIParsingException :: String -> CurrycarbonException

-- | An exection to describe the case that a date is not in the range of
--   the supplied calibration curve
CurrycarbonCalibrationRangeException :: String -> CurrycarbonException
renderCurrycarbonException :: CurrycarbonException -> String
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Utils.CurrycarbonException
instance GHC.Exception.Type.Exception Currycarbon.Utils.CurrycarbonException

module Currycarbon.Parsers

-- | Combine <a>UncalC14</a>, <a>CalPDF</a> and <a>CalC14</a> to render
--   pretty command line output like this:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Sample: 1 ~&gt; [5000±30BP]
--   Calibrated: 3941BC &gt;&gt; 3894BC &gt; 3773BC &lt; 3709BC &lt;&lt; 3655BC
--   1-sigma: 3894-3880BC, 3797-3709BC
--   2-sigma: 3941-3864BC, 3810-3700BC, 3680-3655BC
--                                       ▁▁▁                      
--                                      ▁▒▒▒▁▁    ▁▁▁▁            
--                      ▁▁              ▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁▁▁▒▒▒▒            
--                    ▁▁▒▒             ▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁           
--               ▁▁▁▁▁▒▒▒▒▁           ▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒      ▁▁   
--             ▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁        ▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁   ▁▁▒▒▁  
--           ▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▁▁
--    -3960 ┄─────────┬────────────────┬───────────────┬──────────┄ -3640
--             &gt;       &gt;                   ^          &lt;        &lt;  
--                     ───             ────────────────           
--             ──────────────        ───────────────────   ───── 
--   </pre>
renderCalDatesPretty :: [(UncalC14, CalPDF, CalC14)] -> String
renderCalDatePretty :: (UncalC14, CalPDF, CalC14) -> String
readCalibrationMethod :: String -> Either String CalibrationMethod
parseCalibrationMethod :: Parser CalibrationMethod

-- | Write <a>CalC14</a>s to the file system. The output file is a long
--   .csv file with the following structure:
--   
--   <pre>
--   sample,hdrSigma,hdrStartBCAD,hdrStopBCAD
--   Sample1,1,-3797,-3709
--   Sample1,1,-3894,-3880
--   Sample1,2,-3680,-3655
--   Sample1,2,-3810,-3700
--   Sample1,2,-3941,-3864
--   Sample2,1,-1142,-1130
--   Sample2,1,-1173,-1161
--   Sample2,1,-1293,-1194
--   Sample2,1,-1368,-1356
--   Sample2,2,-1061,-1059
--   Sample2,2,-1323,-1112
--   Sample2,2,-1393,-1334
--   </pre>
writeCalC14s :: FilePath -> [CalC14] -> IO ()
writeCalC14 :: FilePath -> CalC14 -> IO ()
appendCalC14 :: FilePath -> CalC14 -> IO ()
renderCalC14ForFile :: CalC14 -> String
renderCalC14s :: [CalC14] -> String
renderCalC14 :: CalC14 -> String
renderCalRangeSummary :: CalRangeSummary -> String
renderYearBCAD :: YearBCAD -> String
renderHDRsForFile :: [HDR] -> [(String, String)]
renderHDRForFile :: HDR -> (String, String)
renderHDRs :: [HDR] -> String
renderHDR :: HDR -> String
writeCalCurveMatrix :: FilePath -> CalCurveMatrix -> IO ()
renderCalCurveMatrix :: CalCurveMatrix -> String

-- | Write <a>CalPDF</a>s to the file system. The output file is a long
--   .csv file with the following structure:
--   
--   <pre>
--   sample,calBCAD,density
--   ...
--   Sample1,-1391,2.8917924e-4
--   Sample1,-1390,3.3285577e-4
--   Sample1,-1389,3.5674628e-4
--   Sample1,-1388,3.750703e-4
--   ...
--   Sample2,-3678,1.8128564e-3
--   Sample2,-3677,1.9512239e-3
--   Sample2,-3676,2.0227064e-3
--   Sample2,-3675,2.095691e-3
--   ...
--   </pre>
writeCalPDFs :: FilePath -> [CalPDF] -> IO ()
writeCalPDF :: FilePath -> CalPDF -> IO ()
appendCalPDF :: FilePath -> CalPDF -> IO ()
renderCalPDFs :: [CalPDF] -> String
renderCalPDF :: CalPDF -> String
renderCLIPlotCalPDF :: Int -> Int -> CalPDF -> CalC14 -> String
renderUncalC14 :: UncalC14 -> String

-- | Read uncalibrated radiocarbon dates from a file. The file should
--   feature one radiocarbon date per line in the form "&lt;sample
--   name&gt;,&lt;mean age BP&gt;,&lt;one sigma standard deviation&gt;",
--   where &lt;sample name&gt; is optional. A valid file could look like
--   this:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Sample1,5000,30
--   6000,50
--   Sample3,4000,25
--   </pre>
readUncalC14FromFile :: FilePath -> IO [UncalC14]
readUncalC14 :: String -> Either String [UncalC14]
parseUncalC14 :: Parser UncalC14
writeCalCurve :: FilePath -> CalCurveBCAD -> IO ()
renderCalCurve :: CalCurveBCAD -> String

-- | Read a calibration curve file. The file must adhere to the current
--   version of the .c14 file format (e.g. <a>here</a>). Look <a>here</a>
--   for other calibration curves
readCalCurveFromFile :: FilePath -> IO CalCurveBP
readCalCurve :: String -> CalCurveBP
parseCalCurve :: Parser [(YearBP, YearBP, YearRange)]
parseCalCurveLine :: Parser (YearBP, YearBP, YearRange)
comments :: Parser String

module Currycarbon.CalCurves.Intcal20

-- | The intcal20 calibration curve (Reimer et al. 2020, doi:
--   <a>10.1017/RDC.2020.41</a>)
intcal20 :: CalCurveBP

-- | The complete intcal20 calibration curve in a large string
intcal20String :: String

module Currycarbon.Calibration.MatrixMult

-- | Intercept calibration implemented with matrix multiplication (see
--   <a>MatrixMultiplication</a>)
calibrateDateMatrixMult :: Bool -> Bool -> CalCurveBP -> UncalC14 -> Either CurrycarbonException CalPDF

-- | Construct a matrix representation of a calibration curve for a given
--   date
makeCalCurveMatrix :: UncalPDF -> CalCurveBCAD -> CalCurveMatrix

-- | Transform an uncalibrated date to an uncalibrated probability density
--   table
uncalToPDF :: UncalC14 -> UncalPDF

module Currycarbon.Calibration.Bchron

-- | Intercept calibration as implemented in the Bchron R package (see
--   <a>Bchron</a>)
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; :{
--     let calResult = calibrateDateBchron NormalDist False True intcal20 (UncalC14 "" 3000 30)
--         simplifyResult = \(Right (CalPDF _ age dens)) -&gt; VU.zip (VU.take 3 age) (VU.take 3 dens)
--     in simplifyResult calResult
--   :}
--   [(-1413,1.0703295e-5),(-1412,1.4653518e-5),(-1411,1.799142e-5)]
--   </pre>
calibrateDateBchron :: CalibrationDistribution -> Bool -> Bool -> CalCurveBP -> UncalC14 -> Either CurrycarbonException CalPDF

module Currycarbon.Calibration.Calibration

-- | Take an uncalibrated date and a raw calibration curve and return the
--   relevant segment of the calibration curve
getRelevantCalCurveSegment :: UncalC14 -> CalCurveBP -> CalCurveBP

-- | Modify a calibration curve (segment) with multiple optional steps,
--   including interpolation and transforming dates to BC/AD format
prepareCalCurveSegment :: Bool -> CalCurveBP -> CalCurveBCAD

-- | Construct a matrix representation of a calibration curve for a given
--   date
makeCalCurveMatrix :: UncalPDF -> CalCurveBCAD -> CalCurveMatrix

-- | Transform an uncalibrated date to an uncalibrated probability density
--   table
uncalToPDF :: UncalC14 -> UncalPDF

-- | Calibrates a list of dates with the provided calibration curve
calibrateDates :: CalibrateDatesConf -> CalCurveBP -> [UncalC14] -> [Either CurrycarbonException CalPDF]

-- | Transforms the raw, calibrated probability density table to a
--   meaningful representation of a calibrated radiocarbon date
refineCalDates :: [CalPDF] -> [CalC14]
refineCalDate :: CalPDF -> CalC14

-- | A data type to cover the configuration options of the calibrateDates
--   function
data CalibrateDatesConf
CalibrateDatesConf :: CalibrationMethod -> Bool -> Bool -> CalibrateDatesConf

-- | The calibration algorithm that should be used
[_calConfMethod] :: CalibrateDatesConf -> CalibrationMethod

-- | Allow calibration to run outside of the range of the calibration curve
[_calConfAllowOutside] :: CalibrateDatesConf -> Bool

-- | Interpolate the calibration curve before calibration. This is a simple
--   linear interpolation only to increase the output resolution for
--   earlier time periods, where the typical calibration curves are less
--   dense by default. With the interpolation, the output will be a
--   per-year density. The mechanism is inspired by the <a>implementation
--   in the Bchron R package</a>
[_calConfInterpolateCalCurve] :: CalibrateDatesConf -> Bool

-- | A default configuration that should yield almost identical calibration
--   results to the <a>Bchron R package</a>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; defaultCalConf
--   CalibrateDatesConf {_calConfMethod = Bchron {distribution = StudentTDist {ndf = 100.0}}, _calConfAllowOutside = False, _calConfInterpolateCalCurve = True}
--   </pre>
defaultCalConf :: CalibrateDatesConf
instance GHC.Classes.Eq Currycarbon.Calibration.Calibration.CalibrateDatesConf
instance GHC.Show.Show Currycarbon.Calibration.Calibration.CalibrateDatesConf


-- | This module implements an algorithm for the calibration of
--   <a>radiocarbon dates</a>. This is a standard procedure in Archaeology
--   and other fields working with radiocarbon dating.
module Currycarbon

-- | Calibrates a list of dates with the provided calibration curve
calibrateDates :: CalibrateDatesConf -> CalCurveBP -> [UncalC14] -> [Either CurrycarbonException CalPDF]

-- | A data type to cover the configuration options of the calibrateDates
--   function
data CalibrateDatesConf
CalibrateDatesConf :: CalibrationMethod -> Bool -> Bool -> CalibrateDatesConf

-- | The calibration algorithm that should be used
[_calConfMethod] :: CalibrateDatesConf -> CalibrationMethod

-- | Allow calibration to run outside of the range of the calibration curve
[_calConfAllowOutside] :: CalibrateDatesConf -> Bool

-- | Interpolate the calibration curve before calibration. This is a simple
--   linear interpolation only to increase the output resolution for
--   earlier time periods, where the typical calibration curves are less
--   dense by default. With the interpolation, the output will be a
--   per-year density. The mechanism is inspired by the <a>implementation
--   in the Bchron R package</a>
[_calConfInterpolateCalCurve] :: CalibrateDatesConf -> Bool

-- | A default configuration that should yield almost identical calibration
--   results to the <a>Bchron R package</a>
--   
--   <pre>
--   &gt;&gt;&gt; defaultCalConf
--   CalibrateDatesConf {_calConfMethod = Bchron {distribution = StudentTDist {ndf = 100.0}}, _calConfAllowOutside = False, _calConfInterpolateCalCurve = True}
--   </pre>
defaultCalConf :: CalibrateDatesConf

-- | Different calibration algorithms implemented in currycarbon. Currently
--   two distinct implementations are available, although both of them are
--   similar <a>Intercept calibration</a> algorithms. Maybe more algorithms
--   will be added in the future
data CalibrationMethod

-- | A matrix multiplication method generally following <a>this blog post
--   by Martin Hinz</a>. This method is slower and the underlying code more
--   verbose than <a>Bchron</a>, but it has some advantages regarding
--   didactics and the inspection of intermediate data products for
--   debugging. Using this method is thus generally not advisable, except
--   for specific applications, where a more technical insight into C14
--   calibration is needed
MatrixMultiplication :: CalibrationMethod

-- | A fast and reliable calibration algorithm very similar to the
--   implementation in the <a>R package Bchron by Andrew Parnell</a>. This
--   algorithm can be run with a simple normal distribution
--   (<a>NormalDist</a>) or Student's t-distribution (<a>StudentTDist</a>),
--   which is recommended
Bchron :: CalibrationDistribution -> CalibrationMethod
[distribution] :: CalibrationMethod -> CalibrationDistribution

-- | A data type to represent an uncalibrated radiocarbon date
data UncalC14
UncalC14 :: String -> YearBP -> YearRange -> UncalC14

-- | Sample identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_uncalC14Id] :: UncalC14 -> String

-- | C14 age in years BP
[_uncalC14UnCal] :: UncalC14 -> YearBP

-- | C14 standard deviation (one sigma in years)
[_uncalC14Sigma] :: UncalC14 -> YearRange

-- | Read uncalibrated radiocarbon dates from a file. The file should
--   feature one radiocarbon date per line in the form "&lt;sample
--   name&gt;,&lt;mean age BP&gt;,&lt;one sigma standard deviation&gt;",
--   where &lt;sample name&gt; is optional. A valid file could look like
--   this:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Sample1,5000,30
--   6000,50
--   Sample3,4000,25
--   </pre>
readUncalC14FromFile :: FilePath -> IO [UncalC14]

-- | A data type to represent a year-wise probability density for
--   calibrated dates. Although technically not correct, we still call this
--   a probability density function (PDF)
data CalPDF
CalPDF :: String -> Vector YearBCAD -> Vector Float -> CalPDF

-- | Sample identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_calPDFid] :: CalPDF -> String

-- | Years calBCAD
[_calPDFCals] :: CalPDF -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Probability densities for each year in <a>_calPDFCals</a>
[_calPDFDens] :: CalPDF -> Vector Float

-- | Write <a>CalPDF</a>s to the file system. The output file is a long
--   .csv file with the following structure:
--   
--   <pre>
--   sample,calBCAD,density
--   ...
--   Sample1,-1391,2.8917924e-4
--   Sample1,-1390,3.3285577e-4
--   Sample1,-1389,3.5674628e-4
--   Sample1,-1388,3.750703e-4
--   ...
--   Sample2,-3678,1.8128564e-3
--   Sample2,-3677,1.9512239e-3
--   Sample2,-3676,2.0227064e-3
--   Sample2,-3675,2.095691e-3
--   ...
--   </pre>
writeCalPDFs :: FilePath -> [CalPDF] -> IO ()

-- | A type to represent years BP. All numbers are positive and describe
--   the distance in years to 1950AD: 3000 = 3000BP = 1050BC
type YearBP = Word

-- | A type to represent years BC or AD. Negative values describe years BC,
--   positive values years AD: -5000 = 5000BC and 1300 = 1300AD
type YearBCAD = Int

-- | A type to represent a range of years
type YearRange = Word

-- | A data type to represent a calibration curve with <a>YearBP</a>
data CalCurveBP
CalCurveBP :: Vector YearBP -> Vector YearBP -> Vector YearRange -> CalCurveBP

-- | Years calBP
[_calCurveBPCals] :: CalCurveBP -> Vector YearBP

-- | Years BP
[_calCurveBPUnCals] :: CalCurveBP -> Vector YearBP

-- | Standard deviation (one sigma in years)
[_calCurveBPSigmas] :: CalCurveBP -> Vector YearRange

-- | A second data type to represent a calibration curve, here now with
--   <a>YearBCAD</a>
data CalCurveBCAD
CalCurveBCAD :: Vector YearBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD -> Vector YearRange -> CalCurveBCAD

-- | Years calBCAD
[_calCurveBCADCals] :: CalCurveBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Years BCAD
[_calCurveBCADUnCals] :: CalCurveBCAD -> Vector YearBCAD

-- | Standard deviation (one sigma in years)
[_calCurveBCADSigmas] :: CalCurveBCAD -> Vector YearRange

-- | The intcal20 calibration curve (Reimer et al. 2020, doi:
--   <a>10.1017/RDC.2020.41</a>)
intcal20 :: CalCurveBP

-- | Read a calibration curve file. The file must adhere to the current
--   version of the .c14 file format (e.g. <a>here</a>). Look <a>here</a>
--   for other calibration curves
readCalCurveFromFile :: FilePath -> IO CalCurveBP

-- | Transforms the raw, calibrated probability density table to a
--   meaningful representation of a calibrated radiocarbon date
refineCalDates :: [CalPDF] -> [CalC14]

-- | A data type to represent a human readable summary of a calibrated
--   radiocarbon date
data CalC14
CalC14 :: String -> CalRangeSummary -> [HDR] -> [HDR] -> CalC14

-- | Identifier, e.g. a lab number
[_calC14id] :: CalC14 -> String

-- | Summary of the range of the calibrated date
[_calC14RangeSummary] :: CalC14 -> CalRangeSummary

-- | One-sigma high density regions
[_calC14HDROneSigma] :: CalC14 -> [HDR]

-- | Two-sigma high density regions
[_calC14HDRTwoSigma] :: CalC14 -> [HDR]

-- | Write <a>CalC14</a>s to the file system. The output file is a long
--   .csv file with the following structure:
--   
--   <pre>
--   sample,hdrSigma,hdrStartBCAD,hdrStopBCAD
--   Sample1,1,-3797,-3709
--   Sample1,1,-3894,-3880
--   Sample1,2,-3680,-3655
--   Sample1,2,-3810,-3700
--   Sample1,2,-3941,-3864
--   Sample2,1,-1142,-1130
--   Sample2,1,-1173,-1161
--   Sample2,1,-1293,-1194
--   Sample2,1,-1368,-1356
--   Sample2,2,-1061,-1059
--   Sample2,2,-1323,-1112
--   Sample2,2,-1393,-1334
--   </pre>
writeCalC14s :: FilePath -> [CalC14] -> IO ()

-- | Combine <a>UncalC14</a>, <a>CalPDF</a> and <a>CalC14</a> to render
--   pretty command line output like this:
--   
--   <pre>
--   Sample: 1 ~&gt; [5000±30BP]
--   Calibrated: 3941BC &gt;&gt; 3894BC &gt; 3773BC &lt; 3709BC &lt;&lt; 3655BC
--   1-sigma: 3894-3880BC, 3797-3709BC
--   2-sigma: 3941-3864BC, 3810-3700BC, 3680-3655BC
--                                       ▁▁▁                      
--                                      ▁▒▒▒▁▁    ▁▁▁▁            
--                      ▁▁              ▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁▁▁▒▒▒▒            
--                    ▁▁▒▒             ▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁           
--               ▁▁▁▁▁▒▒▒▒▁           ▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒      ▁▁   
--             ▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁        ▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁   ▁▁▒▒▁  
--           ▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▒▁▁▁▒▒▒▒▒▁▁
--    -3960 ┄─────────┬────────────────┬───────────────┬──────────┄ -3640
--             &gt;       &gt;                   ^          &lt;        &lt;  
--                     ───             ────────────────           
--             ──────────────        ───────────────────   ───── 
--   </pre>
renderCalDatesPretty :: [(UncalC14, CalPDF, CalC14)] -> String

module Currycarbon.CLI.RunCalibrate

-- | A data type to represent the options to the CLI module function
--   runCalibrate
data CalibrateOptions
CalibrateOptions :: [UncalC14] -> [FilePath] -> Maybe FilePath -> CalibrationMethod -> Bool -> Bool -> Bool -> Maybe FilePath -> Maybe FilePath -> Maybe FilePath -> Maybe FilePath -> CalibrateOptions

-- | Uncalibrated dates that should be calibrated
[_calibrateUncalC14] :: CalibrateOptions -> [UncalC14]

-- | List of files with uncalibrated dates to be calibrated
[_calibrateUncalC14File] :: CalibrateOptions -> [FilePath]

-- | Path to a .14c file
[_calibrateCalCurveFile] :: CalibrateOptions -> Maybe FilePath

-- | Calibration algorithm that should be used
[_calibrateCalibrationMethod] :: CalibrateOptions -> CalibrationMethod

-- | Allow calibration to run outside of the range of the calibration curve
[_calibrateAllowOutside] :: CalibrateOptions -> Bool

-- | Don't interpolate the calibration curve
[_calibrateDontInterpolateCalCurve] :: CalibrateOptions -> Bool

-- | Suppress the printing of calibration results to the command line
[_calibrateQuiet] :: CalibrateOptions -> Bool

-- | Path to an output file (see CLI documentation)
[_calibrateDensityFile] :: CalibrateOptions -> Maybe FilePath

-- | Path to an output file
[_calibrateHDRFile] :: CalibrateOptions -> Maybe FilePath

-- | Path to an output file
[_calibrateCalCurveSegmentFile] :: CalibrateOptions -> Maybe FilePath

-- | Path to an output file
[_calibrateCalCurveMatrixFile] :: CalibrateOptions -> Maybe FilePath

-- | Interface function to trigger calibration from the command line
runCalibrate :: CalibrateOptions -> IO ()
